volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Persian (Section 2.4.285b - Section 3.1.341)

sections.label: Section 2.4.285b
sections.translation: Assurance !

sections.label: Section 2.4.285c
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I certainly am ; for I am assured that I shall be free ; don ’ t be hoping that you ’ ll ever be so .

sections.label: Section 2.4.287
sections.translation: Can ’ t you cease your impertinence ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.287b
sections.translation: That which you are mentioning , you can ’ t do yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.4.288
sections.translation: Away with you to utter perdition .

sections.label: Section 2.4.288b
sections.original: At tu domum : nam ibi tibi parata praestost .
sections.translation: And off home with you ; for there it ’ s all ready prepared for you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.289
sections.translation: He summons me [*] on my recognizances .

sections.label: Section 2.4.289b
sections.original: Vtinam vades desint , in carcere ut sis .
sections.translation: I only wish the sureties may be out of the way , so that you may get to prison .

sections.label: Section 2.4.290
sections.translation: Why ’ s this ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.290b
sections.translation: Aye , why is it ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.290c
sections.translation: Still abusing me , rascal ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.290d
sections.translation: Why , inasmuch as you are a slave , it ought at least to be allowed a slave to abuse you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.291b
sections.translation: And is it so ? Just look ( holding up his fist .) what I shall give you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.292b
sections.translation: Nothing ; for nothing have you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.292c
sections.translation: May all the Gods and Goddesses confound me ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.293
sections.original: Amicus sum , eveniant volo tibi quae optas .
sections.translation: I am your friend ; I trust that what you wish may befall you ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.293b
sections.translation: and that it may come to pass ;

sections.label: Section 2.4.294
sections.original: nisi te hodie , si prehendero , defigam in terram colaphis .
sections.translation: if I don ’ t this very day , if I lay hold of you , fell you to the ground with blows .

sections.label: Section 2.4.295
sections.original: Tun me defigas ? te cruci ipsum adfigent propediem alii .
sections.translation: if you fell me [*] , may others make you feel yourself fixed to the cross before long .

sections.label: Section 2.4.296
sections.original: Qui te di deaeque — scis quid hinc porro dicturus fuerim ,
sections.translation: But you may the Gods and Goddesses — You understand what I was going to say after that , if I hadn ’ t been able to restrain my tongue . Can ’ t you be off ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.297b
sections.translation: You drive me off with ease ; for already my shadow ’ s getting [*] a whipping in-doors .

sections.label: Section 2.4.298b
sections.translation: May the Gods and Goddesses confound that fellow ! just like a crawling serpent he has got a double tongue , and is a wicked one .

sections.label: Section 2.4.300
sections.original: hercle illum abiisse gaudeo . foris aperit , eccere autem
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I ’ m glad he ’ s gone . ( Going towards the door .) Open , you door . But look ! he ’ s coming from within , the person that I most especially wished to meet with .

sections.label: Section 2.5.302
sections.original: Paratum iam esse dicito , unde argentum sit futurum ,
sections.translation: Tell her that it ’ s now arranged whence the money is to come . Bid her be of good heart ; tell her that I love her exceedingly . When she cheers up , then does she cheer me up . What I ’ ve told you to tell her ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.305
sections.translation: do you quite understand it ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.305b
sections.original: Magis cálleo quam aprugnum callum callet .—
sections.translation: Better than your legs [*] understand you , do I understand it .

sections.label: Section 2.5.306
sections.translation: Make all haste , be off home .

sections.label: Section 2.5.306b
sections.original: Nunc ego huic graphice facetus fiam .
sections.translation: Now I ’ ll make myself a perfect droll towards him ; I ’ ll carry myself with arms a-kimbo , and assume a lordly air [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.5.308
sections.translation: But who ’ s this that ’ s walking like a two-handled jug [*] ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.308b
sections.translation: I ’ ll spit about me in a dignified style .

sections.label: Section 2.5.309
sections.original: Sagaristio hic quidemst . quíd agitur , Sagaristio ? ut valetur ?
sections.translation: Why , surely this is Sagaristio . How are you , Sagaristio ? How do you do ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.310
sections.original: ecquid , quod mandavi tibi , † estne in te speculae ?
sections.translation: Is there any tiny hope in you as to that which I entrusted to you ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.310b
sections.translation: Step this way ; it shall be seen to ; I would have it done . Advance — move forward .

sections.label: Section 2.5.312
sections.original: Quid hoc híc in collo tibi tumet ?
sections.translation: What ’ s this swelling [*] here upon your neck ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.312b
sections.translation: It ’ s a tumour ; forbear to press it , for when any person touches it with a rude hand , pain is the result .

sections.label: Section 2.5.314
sections.translation: When did that first come upon you ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.314b
sections.translation: To-day .

sections.label: Section 2.5.314c
sections.translation: You should order it to be lanced .

sections.label: Section 2.5.315
sections.original: Metuo , ne immaturam secem , ne exhibeat plus negoti .
sections.translation: I ’ m afraid to lance it before it ’ s ripe , lest it should cause me more trouble .

sections.label: Section 2.5.316
sections.translation: I ’ d like to examine your complaint .

sections.label: Section 2.5.316b
sections.original: Ah ah , abi atque cave sis
sections.translation: Be off , and do be careful , will you , of the horns .

sections.label: Section 2.5.317b
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.317c
sections.original: Quia boves bini hic sunt in crumina .
sections.translation: Because a couple of oxen are here in the purse .

sections.label: Section 2.5.318
sections.original: Emitte sodes , ne enices fame ; sine ire pastum .
sections.translation: Do let them out , please ; don ’ t starve them with hunger — do let them go to pasture .

sections.label: Section 2.5.319
sections.original: Enim métuo , ut possim reicere in bubile , ne vagentur .
sections.translation: Why , I ’ m afraid that I mayn ’ t be able to drive them back to their stall , lest they should wander .

sections.label: Section 2.5.320
sections.translation: I ’ ll drive them back ; be of good heart ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.320b
sections.translation: You shall be trusted then ; I ’ ll lend them you Follow this way , please ( taking the purse from his neck .) in this there is the money which you were asking me for a short time since .

sections.label: Section 2.5.322
sections.translation: What is it you say ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.322b
sections.original: Dominus me bovis mercatum Eretriam misit .
sections.translation: My master has sent me to Eretria to purchase some oxen ; at present my Eretria shall be this house of yours .

sections.label: Section 2.5.323b
sections.translation: You speak quite enchantingly ; and I shall very soon return you all the money safe ;

sections.label: Section 2.5.325
sections.original: nam iam omnes sycophantias instruxi et comparavi ,
sections.translation: for now I ’ ve arranged and put in readiness all my devices , in which way I ’ m to get this money out of this Procurer .

sections.label: Section 2.5.326b
sections.translation: So much the better .

sections.label: Section 2.5.327
sections.original: Et mulier ut sit libera atque ipse ultro det argentum .
sections.translation: Both for the damsel to be set at liberty , and , still further , for himself to pay the money . But follow me ; I have need of your assistance in this affair .

sections.label: Section 2.5.328b
sections.translation: Make use of it just as you please .

sections.label: Section 3.1.329
sections.original: Quae res bene vortat mi et tibi et ventri meo
sections.translation: May this same matter turn out well for me , and for yourself , and for my stomach ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.330
sections.translation: and for everlasting victuals for it as well for all time to come ; that I may have more than enough , a superfluity , and that it may outlast me . Follow me this way , my daughter , with the Gods ’ good leave . The matter to which we are to give our attention , you know , you remember , you understand ; to you I have communicated all my designs .

sections.label: Section 3.1.335
sections.original: ea causa ad hoc exemplum te exornavi ego .
sections.translation: For that reason have I dressed you out after this fashion ; young woman , to-day you are to be sold .

sections.label: Section 3.1.336b
sections.translation: Prithee , my dear father , although you do eagerly long for victuals at another ’ s cost , are you for the sake of your appetite going to sell your own daughter ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.339
sections.original: Mirum quin regis Phílippi causa aut Attali
sections.translation: It is a wonder , indeed , if I don ’ t sell you , who are my own , for the sake of King Philip or Attalus [*] , rather than my own .

sections.label: Section 3.1.341
sections.original: Vtrum tú pro ancilla mé habes an pro filia ?
sections.translation: Whether do you regard me as your slave or as your daughter ?
